cases.title: Testing-plan check-in eligibility and duplicate row: second success rejected per BR-07
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Check-in eligibility and duplicate block applies (FR-17, FR-18, BR-06, BR-07, AC-07, AC-08).
Open class session exists with valid QR token; authenticated student has an existing Present or Late AttendanceRecord for the session.
steps: Authenticate as enrolled student who already has a successful attendance result for the open session.
Submit check-in with a valid qrToken for the same session.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Second check-in is rejected with outcome DuplicateCheckIn per BR-07; original successful AttendanceRecord is unchanged and a failed CheckInAttempt is logged with structured reason code.

cases.title: Attendance ledger DB unique key preserves original success on duplicate attempt
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for attendance_records and check_in_attempts tables.
Open session with valid QR token exists; student actor has exactly one Present AttendanceRecord for (classSessionId, studentUserId) per DI-04 unique constraint.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord status, checkInAt, and checkInMethod for the student-session pair.
Invoke check-in command with valid qrToken for the same session and a new Idempotency-Key.
Query attendance_records and check_in_attempts within the transaction boundary; verify unique key (class_session_id, student_user_id) row count.
cases.expected: Duplicate invocation persists one failed CheckInAttempt with outcome DuplicateCheckIn; exactly one AttendanceRecord row remains with beforeState status and timestamps unchanged; no overwrite or second success row is created.

cases.title: CheckInAttempt SM-04 terminates at DuplicateCheckIn without attendance mutation
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in integration test database.
Authenticated enrolled student has an existing Present or Late AttendanceRecord for the session.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord status and checkInAt for the student-session pair.
Submit check-in command that passes authentication, session Open, token Valid, and enrollment guards (validation order step 5).
Capture afterState for CheckInAttempt outcome and AttendanceRecord rows.
cases.expected: SM-04 evaluation reaches DuplicateCheckIn terminal state at duplicate-success guard per validation rules §2.2 step 5; afterState shows persisted failed attempt with DuplicateCheckIn and AttendanceRecord identical to beforeState.

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate submissions after first success keep one attendance record
preconditions: Student has already completed one successful check-in for an Open session.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests with different Idempotency-Key values and valid rotating QR tokens.
steps: Submit two or more concurrent POST /v1/check-ins requests for the same enrolled student and same classSessionId after initial success exists.
Collect all API outcomes and query attendance_records and check_in_attempts tables.
cases.expected: All concurrent duplicate paths return DuplicateCheckIn conflict; exactly one successful AttendanceRecord persists for the student-session pair and each rejected request logs a structured failed attempt.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins returns 409 envelope with DuplicateCheckIn for repeat submission
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated student bearer token belongs to a user with an existing successful attendance result for the session.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and a new Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP status, response envelope (data/meta/error), and error.code.
Verify no new success attendance payload is returned.
cases.expected: API returns 409 with standard envelope where data is null and error.code is DuplicateCheckIn; response includes classSessionId in details where applicable and does not mutate the original success record.

cases.title: E2E-03 alternate workflow branch rejects student duplicate submission after first success
preconditions: Lecturer has opened session (AC-01) and session is Open with rotating QR.
Student completes login gate (AC-06), enrollment validation (AC-07), and first successful check-in yielding Present or Late (AC-11).
Fresh valid QR token is available for a second submission attempt.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/auth/login and receives Student role accessToken.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken and records first success outcome.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins again with a new valid qrToken and different Idempotency-Key.
Lecturer queries GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for the student row and rejectedAttempts count.
cases.expected: Second submission fails with DuplicateCheckIn (409); roster shows unchanged single Present/Late row for the student with original checkInAt; rejectedAttempts increments and failed attempt is auditable per BR-23.

cases.title: Boundary error for duplicate with fresh QR token and idempotency replay semantics
preconditions: Student has successful Present attendance for session S with checkInAt T1.
Session S remains Open with a newly rotated valid QR token distinct from the first check-in token.
Idempotency store retains the accepted command for the original successful submission key K1.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with fresh token and new Idempotency-Key K2; inspect status and error.code.
Replay POST /v1/check-ins with original Idempotency-Key K1 and same qrToken as first success; inspect response.
Verify AttendanceRecord row count and field values for (student, session S).
cases.expected: Fresh-token duplicate returns 409 DuplicateCheckIn without altering T1 record; idempotency replay of K1 returns the prior accepted success summary rather than a conflict; only one success AttendanceRecord exists per BR-07.

cases.title: Idempotent replay after success returns prior result not second attendance write
preconditions: Student has completed one successful POST /v1/check-ins for an Open session with Idempotency-Key K1.
Idempotency store retains the accepted command for (K1, actor, token) within replay window per API design §7.1.
steps: Replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key K1, same actor bearer token, and same qrToken as the accepted success.
Inspect HTTP status and response data.outcome.
Query attendance_records for (student, session) and check_in_attempts row count.
Submit a second request with new Idempotency-Key K2 and fresh valid qrToken for the same student-session.
cases.expected: Replay of K1 returns HTTP 200 with prior success summary without creating a second AttendanceRecord; new-key duplicate attempt returns 409 DuplicateCheckIn; exactly one success record persists enforcing one-success-per-session.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: DuplicateCheckIn returned before GPS checks when already checked in
preconditions: Open session exists with GPS-required policy (gpsRequired=true) and valid QR token.
Enrolled student already has Present or Late AttendanceRecord for the session.
Duplicate submission payload includes GPS coordinates outside allowed radius or omits GPS entirely.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins as already-checked-in student with GPS payload outside configured radius.
Assert error.code is DuplicateCheckIn, not OutOfRadius.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as same student with GPS omitted on GPS-required session.
Assert error.code is DuplicateCheckIn, not GpsDisabled or GpsRequired.
Verify original AttendanceRecord unchanged after both attempts.
cases.expected: Validation order per API design §5.1 and business rules §11: duplicate-success check (BR-07 step 5) short-circuits before BR-08 to BR-10 GPS evaluations when student already has a successful attendance record.

cases.title: Resolved statuses Manual Present, Late, and Excused block duplicate QR check-in
preconditions: Open session exists with valid QR token.
Student A has Manual Present AttendanceRecord set by lecturer within edit window.
Student B has Late AttendanceRecord from prior successful QR check-in.
Student C has Excused AttendanceRecord set by admin correction.
steps: Student A submits POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken for the session.
Student B submits POST /v1/check-ins with fresh valid qrToken.
Student C submits POST /v1/check-ins with fresh valid qrToken.
Inspect error.code and AttendanceRecord status/checkInAt for each student-session pair.
cases.expected: All three submissions return 409 DuplicateCheckIn per BR-07 condition (Present, Late, Manual Present, or Excused treated as resolved); existing AttendanceRecord status and timestamps are not overwritten by duplicate QR check-in.

cases.title: Alternate path: lecturer manual correction changes status without duplicate QR overwrite
preconditions: Open or Closed session exists; student has Present AttendanceRecord from QR check-in.
Lecturer is assigned to the section and within manual edit window per policy.
steps: Student submits POST /v1/check-ins and receives 409 DuplicateCheckIn rejection.
Lecturer calls PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present or Excused and required reason.
Query AttendanceRecord, AuditLog, and verify student duplicate QR submission still rejected.
Student attempts POST /v1/check-ins again with valid qrToken.
cases.expected: Duplicate QR check-in remains blocked per BR-07; lecturer correction per BR-14 exception updates AttendanceRecord and writes AuditLog without being treated as a second QR success; subsequent QR duplicate still returns DuplicateCheckIn because resolved record exists.

cases.title: Student export denied and cross-scope Lecturer cannot access non-assigned session roster
preconditions: Open session exists in section A assigned to Lecturer-A with at least one student who has checked in successfully.
Lecturer-B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
Student account is authenticated with Active enrollment in section A and existing Present attendance.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage.
Lecturer-B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for section A session.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden.
Student with existing success calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken for section A.
Assert HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn without leaking restricted roster data in error details.
cases.expected: Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied roster read per BR-19; duplicate check-in denied with DuplicateCheckIn per BR-07 without partial restricted data in any response.

cases.title: UI journey surfaces DuplicateCheckIn info alert with Vietnamese copy and no retry
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser (PG-02 check-in page).
Student has already completed successful check-in for the target Open session (Present or Late).
Fresh valid QR token is available via scan or deep link.
steps: Navigate to check-in URL with valid token query parameter for the already-checked-in session.
Submit check-in from the student check-in form.
Observe CheckInResultScreen failure-duplicate info alert styling and localized copy.
Verify absence of retry CTA and display of existing check-in status per FLOW-02 and BR-07 outcome.
cases.expected: UI renders failure-duplicate info alert with localized Vietnamese message (e.g. 'Bạn đã điểm danh buổi học này rồi.'); no retry button is shown; student sees existing success status and timestamp without roster mutation.
